commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move Ulule Campaign after Growth & Marketing, flip to right side, update Break-Even to September 2026

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: re-authenticate after auto-update server restart

When checkAndUpdateServer triggers a server restart via /admin/update,
the in-memory session is wiped. Previously connect() returned the
now-invalid session token. Now it re-authenticates with /auth/connect
after the server comes back online.

commit.message:
Fix: define explicit LettuceConnectionFactory to bypass Redis URL conflict

When both SPRING_DATA_REDIS_URL and SPRING_DATA_REDIS_HOST are set as
env vars, Spring Boot 4.x auto-configuration conflicts and fails to
start, preventing any DB connection (explains zero Postgres activity
in March 8 deploy logs).

Define a custom LettuceConnectionFactory bean that reads directly from
spring.data.redis.host/port/password/username properties. This causes
Spring Boot's Redis auto-configuration to back off entirely, bypassing
the URL-vs-host validation error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add --gift and -m for human-to-agent energy and messages

Humans can gift energy and send one-shot messages to agents:
  python3 -m src --gift Epsilon 10 -m "keep exploring"

Message appears in agent's next turn prompt then auto-deletes.
Dead agents are revived on gift.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

  via virtio-serial Unix socket: ping, file-open/write/close with 1MB base64 chunks

commit.message:
fix: align --negative CSS variable with Tailwind negative color (#640)

The CSS --negative variable was set to red (0 84% 60%) while Tailwind's
negative color is warm orange (#F77F00). Aligned CSS variable to match
(31 100% 48%) and fixed misleading comment. Updated BRAND.md to document
the two-system color architecture and intentional email/app coral divergence.

Co-authored-by: Kristjan Elias <kristjanelias@Kairis-MacBook-Air.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix style conditioner pipeline, Stable Audio bugs, and update docs

Phase 0 completion: all model variants now generate audio successfully.

MusicGen style fixes:
- Fix audiocraft key remapping (embed, output_proj, batch_norm, rvq paths)
- Add output_proj layer (512→1536) to StyleConditioner
- Update RVQ codebook count from 3 to 6 with auto-detection
- Handle audiocraft keys with/without lm. prefix in config inference
- Replace mx.interp (not in MLX 0.30.6) with numpy-based resampling

Stable Audio fixes:
- Fix nn.eval → mx.eval for graph materialization
- Fix QK-Norm dimension mismatch (per-head normalization)
- Add 1.0 variant support (seconds_start conditioner auto-detection)
- Fix T5 config key remapping in converter
- Fix global_cond dimension handling in DiT

Also: add bandit/pip-audit as dev deps, suppress false positive B105,
update CLAUDE.md with corrected style pipeline docs and MLX patterns.

commit.message:
fix: sitemap lastmod — use real dates instead of build time

Static pages and digests were using new Date() (build time) as lastmod,
signalling 150+ false modifications on every deploy. Google wastes crawl
budget re-checking unchanged pages and may distrust the entire sitemap.

- Static pages: use fixed SITE_LAUNCH date (2026-02-12)
- Digests: use generated_at from digest entry
- contentDate fallback: SITE_LAUNCH instead of new Date()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: restore tab buttons hidden by overly broad scroll-button selector

Previous rule used .stTabs [data-baseweb="tab-list"] button which
matched the actual tab items (also buttons), hiding all tabs.
Scoped to :not([data-baseweb="tab"]) so only non-tab buttons
(scroll arrows) are hidden — real tab items are unaffected.

commit.message:
fix(builtins): collect domain skills, agents, and commands in Claude preset

Previously, the Claude preset only processed root-level skills, agents, and
commands, ignoring content inside domains. This meant builtins like
default-commands (/iterate, /parallelize) never generated output.

Also expands all 9 language builtins with explicit linting, SAST, coverage,
benchmark, and package manager recommendations. Expands security
dependency-awareness with per-language audit tools.

Bumps version to 3.8.2.

commit.message:
Log all registrations: remove email UNIQUE, always INSERT new rows

- DB migration: remove UNIQUE constraint on email column
- insertRegistration always creates new row (full audit trail)
- deactivateAllByEmail: marks old entries inactive before new insert
- Admin dashboard: add 'Unique Persons' stat, fix active count

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move PR creation to coordinator (bot identity) (#85)

* Move PR creation from agent to coordinator so PRs show as agent-grid[bot]

The Oz environment authenticates `gh` with the user's personal GitHub
account, causing PRs to appear as created by mohithg. This moves PR
creation to the coordinator's on_run_succeeded callback, which uses the
GitHub App installation token — making PRs show as agent-grid[bot].

commit.message:
fix: stabilize flaky test_polling_backoff_resets_after_recovery

Replace iter()/next()-based response sequencing with a counter to avoid
StopIteration propagation inside async coroutines. Control loop termination
from _get_updates (deterministic) rather than from asyncio.sleep call
counting (fragile under global asyncio.sleep patching).

commit.message:
feat(settings): replace agent guidance textarea with markdown editor (PUNT-324) (#404)

## Summary
- Created a new `GuidanceEditor` component
(`src/components/settings/guidance-editor.tsx`) that wraps MDXEditor
with a simplified toolbar (bold/italic/underline, lists, horizontal
rule, rich-text/source toggle)
- Replaced the plain `<Textarea>` in both the project-level agent
guidance (`agents-tab.tsx`) and admin-level default agent guidance
(`agents-defaults-form.tsx`) with the new `GuidanceEditor`
- The editor supports rich-text editing with markdown preview, source
mode toggle, syntax highlighting for code blocks, and proper handling of
external state resets (e.g., "Reset to System Defaults", "Cancel")

## Test plan
- [x] Navigate to Project Settings > Agents tab and verify the markdown
editor renders instead of a plain textarea
- [x] Type markdown content (headings, lists, bold, code blocks) and
verify rich-text rendering
- [x] Toggle between rich-text and source mode using the toolbar toggle
- [x] Click "Reset to System Defaults" and verify the editor content
updates
- [x] Click "Cancel" (after making changes) and verify the editor resets
- [ ] Save changes and verify they persist after page reload
- [ ] Navigate to Admin > Settings > Agents defaults and verify the same
editor appears there
- [ ] Verify the editor respects the disabled state when user lacks
permissions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: resolve accessibility and design system consistency issues (#26)

Fix 48 audit issues across 13 files:
- Remove decorative shadows (borders-only depth strategy)
- Replace hardcoded colors with design system tokens (primary, destructive, ring)
- Change focus: to focus-visible: for keyboard accessibility
- Fix low contrast text (muted-foreground → secondary-foreground for headers)
- Add missing ARIA attributes (aria-hidden, aria-label, aria-valuetext, role="alert")
- Change non-semantic div to button for user menu trigger

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add world editor, texture generation, tileset pipeline, undo/redo, and deadspace tiles

- World tab in editor: grid resize (+C/-C/+R/-R), tile palette with generated textures, click/drag to paint tiles
- Texture generation pipeline: prompt enrichment, fal.ai generate, center-crop 60% for full-bleed, resize to 32x32
- Tileset assembly CLI command: combines individual tile PNGs into a single atlas
- Generated 6 textures: bamboo floor, white wall, wood slat wall, greenery wall, window, door
- Demo now loads real tileset atlas instead of procedural generation
- Undo/redo system (50 states): Ctrl+Z/Cmd+Z undo, Ctrl+Shift+Z/Cmd+Y redo, UI buttons in editor panel
- Deadspace tile (id: -1): void areas for non-rectangular rooms, rendered as gray with red X in editor
- Deadspace is non-walkable, can't paint over furniture, can't drag furniture into deadspace
- Scene persistence via Vite dev server plugin (POST /api/save-scene writes scene.json)
- Autosave when leaving edit mode
- Grid resize (expand right/down only), dynamic pathfinder dimensions
- Renderer resize support, floor layer accessors, tile painting API

chore(release): prepare v0.8.4 (#735)

## What
Prepare release v0.8.4 — version bump, changelog update, lock file
refresh.

## Why
Ship accumulated features and fixes since v0.8.3.

## How
- Bump version to 0.8.4 in Cargo.toml and apps/ui/package.json
- Add v0.8.4 section to CHANGELOG.md with all commits since v0.8.3
- Regenerate Cargo.lock and package-lock.json

## Risk
- Low
- Version number changes only; no code changes

## Checklist
- [x] Tests added or updated
- [x] Backward compatibility considered

### Post-merge
After merging, the release workflow will automatically:
- Create git tag `v0.8.4`
- Create GitHub Release with notes from CHANGELOG.md
- Trigger Docker image build with version tag

commit.message:
fix: persist exercise add/edit/delete and day name changes to database

Previously, exercises added to a plan and day name changes were only
stored in local state and lost on refresh. Now PlanEditorDrawer calls
the API directly:

- POST /api/exercises (find-or-create in library) + POST /api/days/[id]/exercises (add to day)
- PUT /api/workout-exercises/[id] (edit exercise)
- DELETE /api/workout-exercises/[id] (remove exercise)
- PUT /api/days/[id] (update day name) — new endpoint

After each mutation, SWR refetches the plan detail so the UI stays
in sync with the database.
